free_text: Excitation functions for alpha and 3-He particles
induced reactions on zinc
The 66-Zn enriched targets, about 2 mg/cm2 thick,
99.8% isotopic purity, prepared by electro-deposition,
were used for alpha-induced reactions, while
commercially available Zn target foils of natural
abundance, about 13 mg/cm2 thick, 99.9% purity, were
used for 3He-induced reactions. The target assembly
was constructed by these targets and aluminum foils of
appropriate thickness used as energy degraders.
Decay-data
The uncertainties in the determination of cross
sections were estimated to be 8 -10% for the 66-Zn +
alpha and to be 10-20% for the Zn + 3-He reactions
based on those associated with-
Error in current integration by Faraday cup
Error in target thickness
Error in detector efficiency
Error in gamma-ray peak analysis
The correction on cross sections due to the initial
energy spread within the finite thickness of the target
foil in 3He-induced reactions is approx. 10%
Compound nucleus excitation energy
The excitation functions of alpha-induced
reactions have been analyzed based on both the
pre-equilibrium model of Gadioli and hybrid model of
Blann
.The code (DEF) from SF5 was deleted in
Subents 005-008 and subentries 018,019 and 022,
according Memo CP-D/644.
SD: Value 20.9 mb at 21.4 MeV moved from
Subent 012 to 013 (cf. Table 3(a) of J,RCA,46,5,1989)
'AREA' deleted from ANALYSIS.

The uncertainties in the 3He particle energy scale are
as large as +/-1 MeV in the region where cross section
curves steeply rise or fall, as the thick target
assembly employed in this work caused a large
straggling of 3He particles.
The correction on cross sections due to the initial
energy spread within the finite thickness of the target
foil in 3He-induced reactions (~10%), but not those of
the gamma-spectroscopic data given in DECAY-DATA.
